findings.message: Target PR #3 changes exactly the eight approved starter app files and no extra files.
evidence: GitHub changed-file list returned README.md, index.html, package.json, src/data/templates.js, src/lib/safety.js, src/lib/status.js, src/main.js, and src/styles.css.
Target PR head SHA 8b5fb0d006dae82fbf41b1064b8325424a447853 matches the recorded build execution.

findings.message: The target app now uses AG Digitalz as the first internal owned-brand client while keeping platform handles pending.
evidence: src/data/templates.js sets clientName and brandName to AG Digitalz and clientType to internal_owned_brand.
Configured platforms are Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn.
Every configured account has handle not_provided and handleStatus pending_owner_input.

findings.message: The app preserves mandatory blocked states for all live social and automation actions.
evidence: src/data/templates.js keeps draft_only mode, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, schedulingBlocked true, analyticsApiConnected false, credentialsStored false, socialOauthConnected false, and n8nAutomationBlocked true.
src/lib/safety.js asserts AG Digitalz active draft config and rejects provided handles, connected accounts, stored credentials, social OAuth, scheduling, analytics, and live posting.
src/main.js keeps the Blocked Live Actions Panel visible.

findings.message: The app applies the owner-provided content pillars, brand voice, posting target, approval owner, and weekly reporting cadence.
evidence: README.md documents the AG Digitalz internal draft config.
src/data/templates.js includes 8 AG Digitalz content pillars and 7 brand voice traits.
The app states 3 draft post packages per day and 1 weekly report.

After target PR merge, redeploy only the dedicated Netlify staging-only site and record the deploy result.
